    58. TypeError: "x" has no properties

    60. TypeError: can't delete non-configurable array element
    61. RangeError: argument is not a valid code point
    62. TypeError: "x" is not a constructor
    63. TypeError: "x" is not a function
    64. ReferenceError: "x" is not defined
    65. RangeError: precision is out of range
    66. Error: Permission denied to access property "x"
    67. TypeError: "x" is read-only
    68. SyntaxError: redeclaration of formal parameter "x"
    69. TypeError: Reduce of empty array with no initial value
    70. SyntaxError: "x" is a reserved identifier

value.content: This chapter documents all the JavaScript language operators, expressions and keywords.

value.title: Wyrażenia i operatory w podziale na  kategorie
value.titleAsText: Wyrażenia i operatory w podziale na  kategorie

value.title: Lista operatorów w kolejności alfabetycznej znajduje sie w pasku bocznym po lewej stronie.
value.titleAsText: Lista operatorów w kolejności alfabetycznej znajduje sie w pasku bocznym po lewej stronie.

value.title: Wyrażenia podstawowe
value.content:
Podstawowe słowa kluczowe i wyrażenia w  JavaScript.

this
Słowo this odnosi się do kontekstu wywołania.
function
Słowo function umożliwia zdefniowanie wyrażenia funkcji .
class
Słowo class definiuje wyrażenie klasy.
function*
The function* keyword defines a generator function expression.
yield
Pause and resume a generator function
yield*
Delegate to another generator function or iterable object.
[]
Array initializer/literal syntax.
{}
Object initializer/literal syntax.
/ab+c/i
Regular expression literal syntax.
[for (x of y) x]
Array comprehensions.
(for (x of y) y)
Generator comprehensions.
( )
Grouping operator.

value.title: Left-hand-side expressions
value.content:
Left values are the destination of an assignment.

Property accessors
Member operators provide access to a property or method of an object
(object.property and object["property"]).
new
The new operator creates an instance of a constructor.
super
The super keyword calls the parent constructor.
...obj
The spread operator allows an expression to be expanded in places where multiple arguments (for function calls) or multiple elements (for array literals) are expected.

value.title: Increment and decrement
value.content:
Postfix/prefix increment and postfix/prefix decrement operators.

A++
Postfix increment operator.
A--
Postfix decrement operator.
++A
Prefix increment operator.
--A
Prefix decrement operator.

value.title: Unary operators
value.content:
A unary operation is operation with only one operand.

delete
The delete operator deletes a property from an object.
void
The void operator discards an expression's return value.
typeof
The typeof operator determines the type of a given object.
+
The unary plus operator converts its operand to Number type.
-
The unary negation operator converts its operand to Number type and then negates it.
~
Bitwise NOT operator.
!
Logical NOT operator.

value.title: Arithmetic operators take numerical values (either literals or variables) as their operands and return a single numerical value.
value.content:
+
Addition operator.
-
Subtraction operator.
/
Division operator.
*
Multiplication operator.
%
Remainder operator.
value.titleAsText: Arithmetic operators take numerical values (either literals or variables) as their operands and return a single numerical value.

value.title: Operatory porównania
value.content:
Operator porównania, jak nazwa wskazuje, porównuje operandy  i zwraca wartość logiczną zależną od tego, czy wartość porównania jest prawdziwa. 

in
Operator in ustala, czy obiekt posiada daną właściwość.
instanceof
Operator instanceof ustala czy obiekt jest instancją innego obiektu.
<
Less than operator.
>
Greater than operator.
<=
Less than or equal operator.
>=
Greater than or equal operator.

value.title: Equality operators
value.content:
The result of evaluating an equality operator is always of type Boolean based on whether the comparison is true.

==
Equality operator.
!=
Inequality operator.
===
Identity operator.
!==
Nonidentity operator.

value.title: Bitwise shift operators
value.content:
Operations to shift all bits of the operand.

<<
Bitwise left shift operator.
>>
Bitwise right shift operator.
>>>
Bitwise unsigned right shift operator.

value.title: Binary bitwise operators
value.content:
Bitwise operators treat their operands as a set of 32 bits (zeros and ones) and return standard JavaScript numerical values.

&
Bitwise AND.
|
Bitwise OR.
^
Bitwise XOR.

value.title: Binary logical operators
value.content:
Logical operators are typically used with boolean (logical) values, and when they are, they return a boolean value.

&&
Logical AND.
||
Logical OR.

value.title: Conditional (ternary) operator
value.content:
(condition ? ifTrue : ifFalse)

The conditional operator returns one of two values based on the logical value of the condition.

value.title: Assignment operators
value.content:
An assignment operator assigns a value to its left operand based on the value of its right operand.

=
Assignment operator.
*=
Multiplication assignment.
/=
Division assignment.
%=
Remainder assignment.
+=
Addition assignment.
-=
Subtraction assignment
<<=
Left shift assignment.
>>=
Right shift assignment.
>>>= (en-US)
Unsigned right shift assignment.
&=
Bitwise AND assignment.
^=
Bitwise XOR assignment.
|=
Bitwise OR assignment.
[a, b] = [1, 2]
{a, b} = {a:1, b:2}

Destructuring assignment allows you to assign the properties of an array or object to variables using syntax that looks similar to array or object literals.

value.title: Comma operator
value.content:
,
The comma operator allows multiple expressions to be evaluated in a single statement and returns the result of the last expression.

value.title: Non-standard features
value.content:
Legacy generator function
The function keyword can be used to define a legacy generator function inside an expression. To make the function a legacy generator, the function body should contains at least one yield expression.
Expression closures
The expression closure syntax is a shorthand for writing simple function.

value.title: Specifications
value.content:
Specification Status Comment
ECMAScript 1st Edition. Standard Initial definition.
ECMAScript 5.1 (ECMA-262)
The definition of 'Expressions' in that specification.		 Standard  
ECMAScript 2015 (6th Edition, ECMA-262)
The definition of 'ECMAScript Language: Expressions' in that specification.		 Standard New: Spread operator, destructuring assignment, super keyword, Array comprehensions, Generator comprehensions
